matches.match_rationale: The core pain is that by the time HQ sees data, it's 3+ months stale and emerging problems (rising dropout mid-semester) go undetected. Time series forecasting on enrollment, attendance, and assessment data can project trend trajectories in near-real-time, giving HQ leading indicators rather than lagging reports. Education operators typically have enrollment and gradebook data in SIS systems that can feed these models without requiring major new infrastructure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A continuous forecasting layer on top of existing SIS data that predicts end-of-semester dropout risk and performance trajectories at the campus level, surfaced as a weekly HQ dashboard rather than a semester report.

matches.match_rationale: The explicit pain includes the inability to detect and respond to emerging problems like rising dropout mid-semester. Anomaly detection on attendance, gradebook, and engagement signals can flag campuses or cohorts deviating from historical norms in near-real-time, replacing the calendar-driven reporting cycle with event-driven alerts. This directly addresses the 'no ability to detect emerging problems' mechanism without requiring a full dashboard overhaul.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An alert system that monitors weekly campus-level metrics and flags statistical deviations — e.g., a campus with attendance trending 15% below its historical seasonal baseline — routing alerts to the relevant HQ team lead automatically.

matches.match_rationale: The reporting lag is partly a workflow problem: data sits in campus systems and only moves to HQ through a manual, periodic reporting cycle. A task automation agent can orchestrate the end-to-end pipeline — pulling data from SIS/LMS systems, transforming it, populating a central data store, and triggering summary reports — on a weekly or even daily cadence without requiring campus staff to manually compile and submit reports. This removes the human bottleneck that causes the 3-month staleness.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated ETL and reporting agent that ingests campus data on a rolling weekly basis, generates standardized performance summaries per campus, and pushes them to HQ dashboards — replacing the semester-end manual consolidation cycle.

matches.match_rationale: Even when data is available more frequently, HQ leaders often delay action because interpreting raw metrics into narrative context requires analyst time. LLM-generated narrative summaries can transform weekly campus data snapshots into plain-language briefings — 'Campus A has seen a 12% attendance decline over 3 weeks, historically correlated with semester-end dropout spikes' — making data immediately actionable without requiring a data analyst to write it up.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated weekly campus health narratives delivered to HQ leadership, synthesizing metric changes into plain-language summaries with flagged items requiring attention, reducing interpretation lag to zero.
